criteria_results.description: In handleExportProject, no call to zip.folder('assets') exists and no files are written to an assets subfolder inside the ZIP
criteria_results.evidence: useExport.ts lines 97-104: zip only gets zip.file('project.json'), zip.file('document.pdf'), zip.file('overlay.pdf'). No zip.folder('assets') call and no assetsFolder variable anywhere in handleExportProject.

criteria_results.description: A toDataUrl (or equivalent named) async function exists inside handleExportProject in useExport.ts that accepts a string and returns Promise<string>, handling blob:, data:, and plain strings
criteria_results.evidence: useExport.ts lines 30-59: async toDataUrl(content: string) defined inside handleExportProject. blob: handled via fetch+FileReader (lines 38-55), data: and plain strings fall through to return content (line 58).

criteria_results.description: In handleExportProject, finalObjects processing applies toDataUrl to both image type objects and icon type objects whose content is a URL (starts with 'data:' or 'blob:')
criteria_results.evidence: useExport.ts lines 63-73: condition for image (obj.type === 'image' && obj.content) and condition for icon (obj.type === 'icon' && obj.content && (obj.content.startsWith('data:') || obj.content.startsWith('blob:'))) both call toDataUrl(obj.content).

criteria_results.description: In handleExportProject, finalCustomIcons processing applies toDataUrl to each icon's url field instead of processAsset
criteria_results.evidence: useExport.ts lines 78-86: finalCustomIcons map calls toDataUrl(icon.url) at line 82. No processAsset function exists anywhere in the file.

criteria_results.description: In handleZipImport in useImport.ts, the resolvedObjects map handles both image type objects and icon type objects with assets/ content paths
criteria_results.evidence: useImport.ts line 63: condition is (obj.type === 'image' || (obj.type === 'icon' && obj.content.startsWith('assets/'))) — both image and icon types are covered.

criteria_results.description: In handleDirectoryImport in useImport.ts, the resolvedObjects map handles both image type objects and icon type objects with assets/ content paths
criteria_results.evidence: useImport.ts line 135: identical condition (obj.type === 'image' || (obj.type === 'icon' && obj.content.startsWith('assets/'))) present in handleDirectoryImport.

criteria_results.description: The generated project.json inside the ZIP contains no strings starting with 'assets/' — all image and custom icon data is stored as data: URL strings
criteria_results.evidence: useExport.ts: no code writes assets/ paths into projectData. toDataUrl only outputs data: URLs (from blob: conversion) or passes data:/plain strings through unchanged — never produces an assets/ path. projectData is assembled from finalObjects and finalCustomIcons, both processed by toDataUrl. Cannot runtime-verify without actual execution.

suggestions: c7 confidence is MEDIUM because it cannot be verified without actually running an export — consider a manual smoke test: export a project with a custom icon, unzip, grep project.json for 'assets/'.

toDataUrl silently swallows fetch errors for revoked blob: URLs (lines 52-54) and returns the dead blob: URL — downstream PDF embed will then fail silently too; consider logging a user-visible warning.

handleFileImport (useImport.ts line 171) uses an ad-hoc heuristic (length > 1 and not pdf/zip/json) to detect directory uploads — a renamed single non-pdf/zip/json file would incorrectly trigger handleDirectoryImport.
